executive_summary.one_liner: This isn't a command, it's a 437-line monument to over-engineering wrapped in pseudo-psychology

technical_critique: This command violates the PRIME DIRECTIVE: 'Can I solve this with a simple method?' The answer was YES, but instead we got a 437-line fever dream.
Complexity score of 18 when the limit is 5. That's not innovation, that's architectural rebellion.
Template variables (146) outnumber actual executable statements by a factor of infinity (since there are zero executable statements).
The JavaScript pseudo-code is like ordering a burger and getting a recipe card - technically food-related but utterly useless when you're hungry.
functional_critique: Claims to be 'intelligent' but requires manual input for basic data that could be automatically extracted.
Promises 'pattern capture' but stores unstructured data that would make a data scientist weep.
The 'AI-generated priorities' are just more template variables - it's artificial ignorance, not intelligence.
9 separate sections for what could be accomplished with a simple journal entry and basic arithmetic.
user_experience_critique: Estimated 25-45 minute completion time for what should be a 5-minute reflection. That's not ADHD-friendly, that's ADHD torture.
More variables to fill than a tax return, less useful than a fortune cookie.
Claims to reduce cognitive load while simultaneously maximizing it through analysis paralysis.
The irony of an ADHD command that would trigger every ADHD symptom it claims to track.

immediate_actions: Replace template variables with actual executable code or remove them
Create a simple 3-question reflection instead of this bureaucratic nightmare
Test with actual ADHD users (they'll run screaming)
architectural_fixes: Follow the 3-Question Rule: Can I use what exists? (Yes, a text editor)
Implement actual simplicity: Time completed, Energy level, Tomorrow's priority
Remove all pseudo-JavaScript and template madness
Make it executable or make it go away
performance_optimizations: Use actual prompts instead of placeholder pseudo-code
Focus on ONE thing: capturing daily learnings

final_verdict.summary: This command is a masterclass in how NOT to follow CLAUDE.md principles. It's over-engineered, non-functional, cognitively overwhelming, and would make any ADHD person give up before starting.
final_verdict.metaphor: Asking someone with ADHD to complete this reflection is like asking someone with a broken leg to complete a marathon while carrying a piano.
final_verdict.recommendation: EMERGENCY REFACTOR REQUIRED. Current version is more harmful than helpful.
redeeming_qualities: The intention to help ADHD users is noble
Some individual concepts (energy tracking, time estimation) are valid
Could serve as a cautionary tale for future command development
final_verdict.death_sentence: This command needs to be taken behind the barn and put out of its misery, then reborn as something actually useful.

statistical_confidence.bias_disclosure: Biased toward functional software that actually works
